question_text: A motor with a power rating of 4800 W runs for 45 minutes to lift a heavy object. How much total energy does the motor transfer during this time? (Hint: Energy = Power × Time, and convert minutes to seconds)
explanation: Formula: Energy = Power × Time. First convert 45 minutes to seconds: 45 × 60 = 2700 s. Then E = 4800 W × 2700 s = 12,960,000 J. This can also be written as 12.96 MJ (megajoules). The multi-step process and unit conversion make this challenging.
